body.title: Fascia feed: newest messages at bottom, not top
body.intention: I intend that new messages in the device feed appear at the bottom and the natural read direction is top-to-bottom, with auto-scroll following new arrivals.
body.description:
In the Comms fascia Feed box (device's own comms:// channel), messages currently render newest-first (at top). When new messages arrive, scrollTop=scrollHeight scrolls to bottom, creating confusing read direction: old at bottom, new at top, then jump-to-bottom.

Reverse the event array before rendering so newest messages are at the bottom. Keep auto-scroll-to-newest behavior (scrollTop=scrollHeight).

**Affected files:** devices/web_server/server.py (_loadFasciaBox_chat function)
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** IN — reverse event render order, keep auto-scroll. OUT — API order (leave DESC for DB efficiency), other fascia boxes (Status/Console/Settings unaffected).
**Completion criteria:** Open device feed in Comms; messages display with newest at bottom; new messages auto-scroll to bottom; scroll-up shows older messages at top.
